LiD Knowledge-Base Notebook: Berlin

Category Index

  • Constitution & Rights
  • Government & Federalism
  • Elections & Parties
  • Law, Courts & Administration
  • History, Memory & Reunification
  • Europe & Foreign Relations
  • Society, Family, Education & Work
  • Symbols, Geography & State Facts

Constitution & Rights

  1. Germany's constitution is called the Grundgesetz.

    Keywords: Grundgesetz, Verfassung
    Questions: 6,11

  2. Article 1 of the Grundgesetz guarantees the inviolability of human dignity.

    Keywords: Artikel 1, Menschenwürde
    Questions: 18

  3. Meinungsfreiheit is a Grundrecht and lets people openly express opinions, including in reader letters and criticism of government.

    Keywords: Meinungsfreiheit, Grundrecht, Leserbriefe
    Questions: 1,4,14

  4. Pressefreiheit is a Grundrecht and cannot be abolished by a Bundestag party.

    Keywords: Pressefreiheit, Grundrecht, Bundestag
    Questions: 12

  5. Meinungsfreiheit is limited when false claims about individual persons are publicly spread.

    Keywords: Meinungsfreiheit, Einschränkung, falsche Behauptungen
    Questions: 16

  6. Glaubens- und Gewissensfreiheit is a constitutional Grundrecht.

    Keywords: Glaubensfreiheit, Gewissensfreiheit, Grundrecht
    Questions: 7

  7. Asyl is the Grundrecht in Germany that applies only to foreigners.

    Keywords: Asyl, Ausländerinnen, Ausländer, Grundrecht
    Questions: 9

  8. Freizügigkeit means people may choose their own place of residence.

    Keywords: Freizügigkeit, Wohnort
    Questions: 19

  9. The Grundgesetz forbids Zwangsarbeit, while Geldstrafe is compatible with it.

    Keywords: Zwangsarbeit, Geldstrafe, Grundgesetz
    Questions: 10,15

  10. Germany's equality principle forbids state discrimination and unequal treatment, including disability discrimination.

    Keywords: Gleichbehandlung, Ungleichbehandlung, Behinderung
    Questions: 17,262

  11. The Grundgesetz does not promise that everyone should have the same amount of money.

    Keywords: Grundgesetz, Gleichheit, Geld
    Questions: 8

  12. In Germany, state and religious communities are separate.

    Keywords: Staat, Religion, Trennung
    Questions: 33

  13. German society follows religious tolerance: each person may believe what they want.

    Keywords: religiöse Toleranz, Glauben
    Questions: 292

  14. Briefgeheimnis means intentionally opening a letter addressed to someone else violates protected correspondence.

    Keywords: Briefgeheimnis, Post, Rechte
    Questions: 274

Government & Federalism

  1. Germany is a republic, not a monarchy.

    Keywords: Republik, Monarchie, Staatsform
    Questions: 22,34

  2. Germany is a democratic, social Bundesstaat.

    Keywords: demokratisch, sozial, Bundesstaat
    Questions: 26,27,38

  3. Volkssouveränität means all state power comes from the people.

    Keywords: Volkssouveränität, Staatsgewalt, Volk
    Questions: 52,61

  4. In a Rechtsstaat, residents and the state must obey the laws.

    Keywords: Rechtsstaat, Gesetze, Staat
    Questions: 3,53

  5. Gewaltenteilung separates Legislative, Exekutive, and Judikative; Presse and Direktive are not state powers.

    Keywords: Gewaltenteilung, Legislative, Exekutive, Judikative
    Questions: 32,54,60

  6. Bundestag and Bundesrat belong to the Legislative.

    Keywords: Bundestag, Bundesrat, Legislative
    Questions: 60

  7. Courts and judges belong to the Judikative, the rechtsprechende Gewalt.

    Keywords: Judikative, Richter, rechtsprechende Gewalt
    Questions: 143,144,145

  8. Germany has 16 Bundesländer, each with its own government.

    Keywords: Bundesländer, Regierung, Föderalismus
    Questions: 24,39

  9. The Federal Republic is organized into Bund, Länder, and Kommunen, with Gemeinden as the lowest political level.

    Keywords: Bund, Länder, Kommunen, Gemeinden
    Questions: 64,69

  10. School policy is mainly decided by the Bundesländer, and the state controls schools because the Grundgesetz assigns it that task.

    Keywords: Schulpolitik, Bundesländer, Schulwesen
    Questions: 49,67,68

  11. The Bundestag is the parliament for all of Germany, elected by the eligible people.

    Keywords: Bundestag, Parlament, wahlberechtigtes Volk
    Questions: 28,74

  12. Parliament decides new laws in Germany.

    Keywords: Parlament, Gesetzgebung, Gesetz
    Questions: 42

  13. The Bundestag does not form the Bundeskabinett.

    Keywords: Bundestag, Bundeskabinett, Aufgaben
    Questions: 65

  14. The Bundesrat is formed by government representatives of the Bundesländer and lets the Länder participate in federal legislation.

    Keywords: Bundesrat, Bundesländer, Gesetzgebung
    Questions: 85,90

  15. A state government change can affect federal politics if it changes the Bundesrat majority.

    Keywords: Bundesrat, Mehrheit, Bundespolitik
    Questions: 91

  16. The Bundespräsident is Germany's Staatsoberhaupt and mainly represents the country.

    Keywords: Bundespräsident, Staatsoberhaupt, Repräsentation
    Questions: 84,87

  17. The Bundesversammlung elects the Bundespräsident; citizens do not directly elect that office.

    Keywords: Bundesversammlung, Bundespräsident, direkte Wahl
    Questions: 44,86

  18. The Bundespräsident appoints federal ministers and proposes the Chancellor for election.

    Keywords: Bundespräsident, Minister, Kanzlerwahl
    Questions: 58,70

  19. The Bundestag elects the Bundeskanzlerin/den Bundeskanzler, who leads the Bundeskabinett.

    Keywords: Bundestag, Bundeskanzler, Bundeskabinett
    Questions: 81,82,83

  20. The Chancellor works most often in Berlin because the Bundeskanzleramt and Bundestag are there.

    Keywords: Berlin, Bundeskanzleramt, Bundestag
    Questions: 71

Elections & Parties

  1. Wahlen in Germany are free, equal, and secret, and no voter may be forced or disadvantaged for their vote.

    Keywords: Wahlen, frei, gleich, geheim
    Questions: 5,112,122

  2. Voting in democratic elections is a right, and aktives Wahlrecht means a person can vote.

    Keywords: aktives Wahlrecht, Wahlrecht, Recht
    Questions: 114,115,116

  3. Bundestagswahl voters must be citizens of the Federal Republic and at least 18 years old.

    Keywords: Bundestagswahl, 18, Staatsbürger
    Questions: 94,108

  4. The Bundestag is elected for four years, so Bundestagswahlen normally happen every four years.

    Keywords: Bundestag, vier Jahre, Bundestagswahl
    Questions: 107,109,110

  5. The Bundestagswahl elects the parliament for Germany.

    Keywords: Bundestagswahl, Bundestag, Parlament
    Questions: 124,129

  6. The German federal electoral system combines majority and proportional representation.

    Keywords: Wahlsystem, Mehrheitswahl, Verhältniswahl
    Questions: 120

  7. More Zweitstimmen give a party more parliamentary seats.

    Keywords: Zweitstimmen, Sitze, Parlament
    Questions: 93

  8. The 5%-Hürde is the minimum vote share to enter parliament and exists because many small parties can make government formation harder.

    Keywords: 5%-Hürde, Mindestanteil, Regierungsbildung
    Questions: 117,121,123,127

  9. Before an election, eligible voters receive a Wahlbenachrichtigung from the municipality.

    Keywords: Wahlbenachrichtigung, Gemeinde, Wahl
    Questions: 126

  10. Briefwahl is allowed in Bundestags- and Landtagswahlen.

    Keywords: Briefwahl, Bundestagswahl, Landtagswahl
    Questions: 133

  11. Wahlhelferinnen and Wahlhelfer count votes after the election and may be a required honorary service for German citizens.

    Keywords: Wahlhelfer, Ehrenamt, Stimmen zählen
    Questions: 105,106,282

  12. A Landtagswahl is the election of a German state parliament.

    Keywords: Landtagswahl, Bundesland, Parlament
    Questions: 62

  13. A mayor is the head of a Gemeinde.

    Keywords: Bürgermeister, Gemeinde, Kommunalpolitik
    Questions: 131

  14. Germany has multiple parties so different citizen opinions are represented.

    Keywords: Parteien, Demokratie, Meinungen
    Questions: 41

  15. A party can be banned if it fights the constitution or aims to establish a dictatorship.

    Keywords: Parteiverbot, Verfassung, Diktatur
    Questions: 20,43

  16. A coalition is the cooperation of parties to form a government.

    Keywords: Koalition, Regierung, Parteien
    Questions: 31

  17. Opposition means the MPs not belonging to the governing party or parties, and it controls the government.

    Keywords: Opposition, Abgeordnete, Regierungskontrolle
    Questions: 13,88

  18. A Fraktion is the association of a party's MPs in parliament.

    Keywords: Fraktion, Abgeordnete, Parlament
    Questions: 89

Law, Courts & Administration

  1. The Bundesverfassungsgericht interprets the Grundgesetz.

    Keywords: Bundesverfassungsgericht, Grundgesetz, Auslegung
    Questions: 80

  2. A court procedure is called a Prozess, and criminal proceedings happen when someone is accused after committing a crime.

    Keywords: Prozess, Gericht, Straftat
    Questions: 139,146

  3. Judges work at courts, speak judgments, and Recht sprechen.

    Keywords: Richter, Urteil, Recht sprechen
    Questions: 142,147

  4. Schöffinnen and Schöffen are honorary judges who decide with professional judges about guilt and punishment.

    Keywords: Schöffe, ehrenamtlicher Richter, Schuld, Strafe
    Questions: 140,150

  5. Rechtsanwältinnen and Rechtsanwälte advise on legal questions and represent people in court.

    Keywords: Rechtsanwalt, Rechtsberatung, Gericht
    Questions: 141

  6. The Arbeitsgericht handles workplace conflicts, including unjustified dismissal.

    Keywords: Arbeitsgericht, Kündigung, Arbeitswelt
    Questions: 136,137,138

  7. Police monitor compliance with laws.

    Keywords: Polizei, Gesetze, Überwachung
    Questions: 148

  8. Ordnungsamt is an office belonging to municipal administration.

    Keywords: Ordnungsamt, Gemeindeverwaltung, Verwaltung
    Questions: 56

  9. When moving in Germany, registration is done at the Einwohnermeldeamt.

    Keywords: Einwohnermeldeamt, Umzug, Anmeldung
    Questions: 253

  10. To protest a wrong tax assessment or wrong official bill, file Einspruch or Widerspruch with the authority.

    Keywords: Steuerbescheid, Einspruch, Widerspruch, Behörde
    Questions: 280,283

  11. If badly treated by a public authority contact person, complain to the Behördenleiterin or Behördenleiter.

    Keywords: Behörde, Beschwerde, Behördenleitung
    Questions: 276

  12. Opening a restaurant requires a Gaststättenerlaubnis from the competent authority.

    Keywords: Gaststättenerlaubnis, Restaurant, Behörde
    Questions: 256

  13. A broken purchased television can be reklamiert.

    Keywords: Reklamation, Kauf, Verbraucher
    Questions: 290

History, Memory & Reunification

  1. Hitler became Reichskanzler in 1933, and the National Socialists ruled from 1933 to 1945.

    Keywords: Hitler, Reichskanzler, Nationalsozialisten, 1933 bis 1945
    Questions: 152,155,156

  2. The NS regime created a dictatorship called the Dritte Reich.

    Keywords: Drittes Reich, Diktatur, NS-Staat
    Questions: 157,158

  3. Free elections and parties did not exist under National Socialism.

    Keywords: Nationalsozialismus, freie Wahlen, Parteiverbot
    Questions: 159,170

  4. The NS state was characterized by state racism.

    Keywords: NS-Staat, Rassismus, Nationalsozialismus
    Questions: 161

  5. The Second World War lasted from 1939 to 1945 and ended in Europe on 8 May 1945.

    Keywords: Zweiter Weltkrieg, 1939, 1945, 8. Mai
    Questions: 153,154,160

  6. The Second World War in Europe officially ended with Germany's unconditional surrender.

    Keywords: Kapitulation, Zweiter Weltkrieg, 1945
    Questions: 179

  7. On 9 November 1938, National Socialists and supporters destroyed Jewish shops and synagogues.

    Keywords: 9. November 1938, Synagogen, jüdische Geschäfte
    Questions: 163,164

  8. Holocaust denial is antisemitic behavior and can be punished with up to five years' imprisonment or a fine.

    Keywords: Holocaust, Antisemitismus, Freiheitsstrafe, Geldstrafe
    Questions: 96,149

  9. Stolpersteine commemorate the victims of National Socialism.

    Keywords: Stolpersteine, Opfer, Nationalsozialismus
    Questions: 206

  10. Claus Schenk Graf von Stauffenberg is known for the 20 July 1944 assassination attempt on Hitler.

    Keywords: Stauffenberg, 20. Juli 1944, Attentat
    Questions: 162

  11. After the Second World War, Germany had four occupation zones run by the USA, Soviet Union, Great Britain, and France.

    Keywords: Besatzungszonen, Alliierte, USA, Sowjetunion, Großbritannien, Frankreich
    Questions: 167,175,176

  12. Berlin was divided into four sectors after the Second World War.

    Keywords: Berlin, vier Sektoren, Besatzung
    Questions: 177

  13. The Berlin Airlift supplied West Berlin from June 1948 to May 1949 because the Soviet Union blocked all land routes.

    Keywords: Luftbrücke, West-Berlin, Sowjetunion, Blockade
    Questions: 178

  14. The Federal Republic of Germany was founded in 1949, with Konrad Adenauer as first Chancellor.

    Keywords: Bundesrepublik Deutschland, 1949, Konrad Adenauer
    Questions: 169,165,180

  15. The Wirtschaftswunder in West Germany was in the 1950s.

    Keywords: Wirtschaftswunder, 50er Jahre
    Questions: 183

  16. The DDR was founded in 1949 in the Soviet occupation zone and existed from 1949 to 1990.

    Keywords: DDR, 1949, 1990, sowjetische Besatzungszone
    Questions: 172,174,217

  17. DDR means Deutsche Demokratische Republik and had a planned economy.

    Keywords: DDR, Deutsche Demokratische Republik, Planwirtschaft
    Questions: 190,203

  18. The DDR belonged to the Warschauer Pakt in the Cold War.

    Keywords: DDR, Kalter Krieg, Warschauer Pakt
    Questions: 202,207

  19. The Stasi was the DDR secret service, formally the Ministry for State Security.

    Keywords: Stasi, DDR, Geheimdienst, Ministerium für Staatssicherheit
    Questions: 199,208

  20. The DDR built the Berlin Wall in 1961, dividing Berlin until it opened in 1989.

    Keywords: Berliner Mauer, DDR, 1961, 1989
    Questions: 151,188,189,191,193

  21. On 17 June 1953, the DDR saw nationwide strikes and a popular uprising.

    Keywords: 17. Juni 1953, DDR, Volksaufstand
    Questions: 186,210

  22. In the 1989 Montagsdemonstrationen in the DDR, people shouted Wir sind das Volk.

    Keywords: Montagsdemonstrationen, Wir sind das Volk, DDR, 1989
    Questions: 166

  23. Die Wende means the DDR changed politically from dictatorship to democracy in autumn 1989.

    Keywords: Die Wende, DDR, 1989, Demokratie
    Questions: 196

  24. German reunification happened when the five eastern Bundesländer joined the Federal Republic; Helmut Kohl is the Kanzler der Deutschen Einheit.

    Keywords: Deutsche Wiedervereinigung, neue Bundesländer, Helmut Kohl
    Questions: 204,215,218,228

Europe & Foreign Relations

  1. Germany is a founding member of the European Union.

    Keywords: Deutschland, EU, Gründungsmitglied
    Questions: 173

  2. EU means Europäische Union, and European integration means European states joining together in the EU.

    Keywords: EU, Europäische Union, europäische Integration
    Questions: 224,231

  3. The EU has 27 member states today.

    Keywords: EU, Mitgliedstaaten, 27
    Questions: 236

  4. The Römischen Verträge founded the Europäische Wirtschaftsgemeinschaft and joined West Germany with other states in it.

    Keywords: Römische Verträge, EWG, Europäische Wirtschaftsgemeinschaft
    Questions: 237,239

  5. Germany has used euro cash since 2002.

    Keywords: Euro, Bargeld, 2002
    Questions: 240

  6. The Schengener Abkommen lets Germans travel to many European countries without passport control.

    Keywords: Schengen, Passkontrolle, Europa
    Questions: 221

  7. The European Parliament is elected every five years.

    Keywords: Europäisches Parlament, Europawahl, 5 Jahre
    Questions: 230,232

  8. The European Parliament works in Strasbourg, Luxembourg, and Brussels.

    Keywords: Europäisches Parlament, Straßburg, Luxemburg, Brüssel
    Questions: 234,238

  9. A central EU goal shown by Kohl and Mitterrand at Verdun is peace and security in EU countries.

    Keywords: EU, Verdun, Frieden, Sicherheit
    Questions: 235

  10. Germany's special responsibility for Israel is based on the Nazi crimes against Jews.

    Keywords: Israel, Verantwortung, nationalsozialistische Verbrechen
    Questions: 288

  11. In Germany, publicly calling for the destruction of Israel is forbidden.

    Keywords: Israel, Verbot, Vernichtung Israels
    Questions: 111

Society, Family, Education & Work

  1. Parents may decide until a child turns 14 whether the child attends religious education at school.

    Keywords: Eltern, 14, Religionsunterricht
    Questions: 2

  2. Most children in Germany are subject to Schulpflicht and Anwesenheitspflicht at school.

    Keywords: Schulpflicht, Anwesenheitspflicht, Kinder
    Questions: 95,260

  3. Parents or guardians decide whether a child goes to Kindergarten.

    Keywords: Kindergarten, Eltern, Erziehungsberechtigte
    Questions: 242

  4. Children from age three until starting school have a claim to a Kindergarten place.

    Keywords: Kindergartenplatz, drei Jahre, Ersteinschulung
    Questions: 269

  5. Abitur is normally needed to begin university study, and adults can complete it at an Abendgymnasium.

    Keywords: Abitur, Universität, Abendgymnasium
    Questions: 244,257,261

  6. Volkshochschule is an institution for Weiterbildung.

    Keywords: Volkshochschule, Weiterbildung
    Questions: 270

  7. The Berufsinformationszentrum BIZ at the Bundesagentur für Arbeit helps with apprenticeship searches.

    Keywords: BIZ, Bundesagentur für Arbeit, Lehrstellensuche
    Questions: 259

  8. Full legal adulthood in Germany begins at 18.

    Keywords: volljährig, 18
    Questions: 246

  9. Young people are strafmündig from age 14 and can be punished for criminal offenses.

    Keywords: strafmündig, Jugendliche, 14
    Questions: 263

  10. Child-raising is mainly the parents' responsibility.

    Keywords: Kindererziehung, Eltern, Familie
    Questions: 248,249

  11. Hitting a child in Germany can be punished, and Jugendamt can remove a child who is beaten or left hungry.

    Keywords: Kinderschutz, Jugendamt, Strafe
    Questions: 251,258

  12. For child-raising problems, parents can get help from the Jugendamt.

    Keywords: Jugendamt, Erziehungsprobleme, Eltern
    Questions: 255,273

  13. Mutterschutz is the special legal protection shortly before and after childbirth.

    Keywords: Mutterschutz, Schwangerschaft, Geburt
    Questions: 247

  14. To receive Elterngeld, parents must apply at the Elterngeldstelle.

    Keywords: Elterngeld, Elterngeldstelle, Antrag
    Questions: 241

  15. Couples marry first at the Standesamt, and German law allows marriage to only one partner at a time.

    Keywords: Standesamt, Ehe, Monogamie
    Questions: 252,265,272

  16. Divorce usually requires the Trennungsjahr and legal support from a lawyer.

    Keywords: Scheidung, Trennungsjahr, Anwalt
    Questions: 254,275

  17. Adults may choose their own partners; parents must respect a full-age daughter's decision.

    Keywords: Volljährigkeit, Partnerschaft, Eltern
    Questions: 267

  18. A 13-year-old and a 25-year-old may not live together as a couple.

    Keywords: Jugendschutz, Paar, 13 Jahre
    Questions: 245

  19. Most employed people in Germany work for a company or public authority.

    Keywords: Erwerbstätige, Firma, Behörde
    Questions: 23

  20. Good training gives the best chances for a well-paid job, and adults must keep learning after training.

    Keywords: Ausbildung, Arbeitsplatz, Weiterbildung
    Questions: 250,284

  21. Most social insurance is financed through Sozialabgaben and paid by employers and employees.

    Keywords: Sozialversicherung, Sozialabgaben, Arbeitgeber, Arbeitnehmer
    Questions: 35,97,99

  22. Krankenversicherung and Pflegeversicherung are part of social security or social insurance, but Lebensversicherung is not statutory social insurance.

    Keywords: Krankenversicherung, Pflegeversicherung, Lebensversicherung
    Questions: 36,45,100

  23. Unions represent workers, and a Betriebsrat helps employees with employer problems inside a company.

    Keywords: Gewerkschaften, Betriebsrat, Arbeitnehmer
    Questions: 101,135,286

  24. Employees ending an employment relationship must observe the Kündigungsfrist.

    Keywords: Kündigungsfrist, Arbeitsverhältnis, Kündigung
    Questions: 287

  25. A pregnant employee may not be dismissed because she is having a child.

    Keywords: Schwangerschaft, Kündigung, Diskriminierung
    Questions: 104

  26. Job discrimination includes rejecting someone only because she is a mother, uses a wheelchair, has dark skin, or is denied pool entry because of dark skin.

    Keywords: Diskriminierung, Gleichbehandlung, Mutter, Rollstuhl, Hautfarbe
    Questions: 277,278,281,289

  27. Demonstrations on public streets must be registered beforehand.

    Keywords: Demonstration, anmelden, Straße
    Questions: 243

  28. Citizens can support or found a Bürgerinitiative to preserve a local bus line.

    Keywords: Bürgerinitiative, Beteiligung, Buslinie
    Questions: 134

  29. Ehrenamt means voluntary and unpaid work in clubs and associations.

    Keywords: Ehrenamt, Vereine, Verbände
    Questions: 132

  30. Church membership matters on tax declarations because Kirchensteuer is linked to income and wage tax.

    Keywords: Kirchensteuer, Steuererklärung, Kirche
    Questions: 291

Symbols, Geography & State Facts

  1. Germany's full name is Bundesrepublik Deutschland.

    Keywords: Bundesrepublik Deutschland, Staatsname
    Questions: 212

  2. Germany has about 84 million inhabitants.

    Keywords: Einwohner, Deutschland, 84 Millionen
    Questions: 213

  3. The German flag is schwarz-rot-gold.

    Keywords: Flagge, schwarz-rot-gold
    Questions: 214

  4. The national anthem begins with Einigkeit und Recht und Freiheit.

    Keywords: Nationalhymne, Einigkeit, Recht, Freiheit
    Questions: 40

  5. Germany's heraldic animal is the Adler, and the Bundesadler appears in the Bundestag plenary hall.

    Keywords: Adler, Bundesadler, Bundestag
    Questions: 29,216

  6. The Bundeswehr is the German army.

    Keywords: Bundeswehr, Armee
    Questions: 77

  7. The Bundesverdienstkreuz honors special achievements in political, economic, cultural, intellectual, or social fields.

    Keywords: Bundesverdienstkreuz, Auszeichnung, Leistung
    Questions: 102

  8. Germany's economic system is the soziale Marktwirtschaft, where the economy follows supply and demand while the state provides social balance.

    Keywords: soziale Marktwirtschaft, Angebot, Nachfrage, sozialer Ausgleich
    Questions: 50,171

  9. Elsass-Lothringen is not a Bundesland.

    Keywords: Bundesländer, Elsass-Lothringen
    Questions: 25

  10. Brandenburg, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Sachsen, Sachsen-Anhalt, and Thüringen belonged to the former DDR area.

    Keywords: neue Bundesländer, DDR, Brandenburg, Sachsen, Thüringen
    Questions: 192,195,197,198,200,201

  11. Germany has had its current borders since 1990.

    Keywords: Grenzen, 1990, Deutschland
    Questions: 219

  12. Tag der Deutschen Einheit is celebrated on 3 October.

    Keywords: 3. Oktober, Deutsche Einheit, Feiertag
    Questions: 194

  13. The current German Chancellor in the pack is Friedrich Merz.

    Keywords: Bundeskanzler, Friedrich Merz
    Questions: 72

  14. The current German head of state in the pack is Frank-Walter Steinmeier.

    Keywords: Staatsoberhaupt, Bundespräsident, Frank-Walter Steinmeier
    Questions: 75

  15. The pack names CDU/CSU and AfD as the two largest Bundestag parliamentary groups.

    Keywords: Bundestag, Fraktionen, CDU/CSU, AfD
    Questions: 73

  16. CDU means Christlich Demokratische Union, CSU means Christlich Soziale Union, SPD means Sozialdemokratische Partei Deutschlands, and FDP means Freie Demokratische Partei.

    Keywords: CDU, CSU, SPD, FDP, Parteien
    Questions: 76,78,79,92

  17. An Ampelkoalition is cooperation by SPD, FDP, and Bündnis 90/Die Grünen in a government.

    Keywords: Ampelkoalition, SPD, FDP, Bündnis 90/Die Grünen
    Questions: 103

  18. Berlin is a Stadtstaat, and its state head of government is the Regierende Bürgermeisterin/der Regierende Bürgermeister.

    Keywords: Berlin, Stadtstaat, Regierender Bürgermeister
    Questions: BE-7,BE-9

  19. Berlin's Landesparlament is elected for five years.

    Keywords: Berlin, Landesparlament, 5 Jahre
    Questions: BE-3

  20. In Berlin Kommunalwahlen for the Bezirksverordnetenversammlung, voting starts at age 16.

    Keywords: Berlin, Kommunalwahl, Bezirksverordnetenversammlung, 16
    Questions: BE-4

  21. Berlin's state flag colors are weiß-rot.

    Keywords: Berlin, Landesflagge, weiß-rot
    Questions: BE-5

  22. Pankow is a Berlin district.

    Keywords: Berlin, Bezirk, Pankow
    Questions: BE-2

  23. In Berlin, people can get political information from the Landeszentrale für politische Bildung.

    Keywords: Berlin, politische Bildung, Landeszentrale
    Questions: BE-6

  24. Berlin does not have a Senatorin/Senator für Außenbeziehungen.

    Keywords: Berlin, Senat, Außenbeziehungen
    Questions: BE-10
